revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Fujiuchi-jinja]][[ja:藤内神社]] {{Expand Japanese|藤内神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Fujiuchi Shrine | native_name = 藤内神社 | image = Fujiuchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Fujiuchi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.446333|140.402167}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Fujiuchi Shrine'''|藤内神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hitachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * Province}} ** Series ordinal: 19 ** P155: {{ill|Sakatsura Isosaki Shrine|lt=Sakatsura Isosaki Shrine|en|Sakatsura Isosaki Shrine|ja|酒列磯前神社|simple|Sakatsura Isosaki Shrine|zh|酒列磯前神社|qid=Q11644289|1=Sakatsura Isosaki Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039767|ltq=Q135039767|qid=Q135039767|1=Ihafuneno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * Hitachi|ru|Хитати (провинция)|simple|Hitachi Province|sk|Hitači (provincia)|sr|Провинција Хитачи|tr|Hitachi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Хітаті|zh_min_nan|Hitati-kok|zh_yue|常陸國|zh|常陸國|qid=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}} * {{ill|Q1062968|ltq=Q1062968|qid=Q1062968|1=Naka District, Ibaraki}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.446333|140.402167}} == image (P18) == * Fujiuchi Jinja Haiden.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181772 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|Q7311343|ltq=Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == postal code (P281) == * 311-4201https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7050005000793 == Commons category (P373) == * Fujiuchi-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Fuchiuchino Shrine|lt=Fuchiuchino Shrine|qid=Q135039766|1=Fuchiuchino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '茨城県水戸市藤井町874', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Fujiuchi-jinja}} [[JA:藤内神社]] {{wikidata link|Q66764741}} == Japanese content == {{?????|date=2019?8?28? (?) 02:04 (UTC)}} {{?? |?? = Fujiuchi Shrine |??=[[File:Fujiuchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |??? = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mito|Q204249|qid=Q204249|1=Mito, Ibaraki}} Fujii-ch? 874 |?? = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |?? = {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} |?? = |?? = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |?? = {{ill|lt=Y?r?|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=721|Q23541|qid=Q23541|1=721}}) |?? = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |?? = |??? = |?? = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|qid=Q2502|1=April 10}} |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=????|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{????}} [[File:Fujiuchi Jinja Torii.jpg|250px|thumb|
Torii
]] '''Fujiuchi Shrine''' (????????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mito|Q204249|qid=Q204249|1=Mito, Ibaraki}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === *'''{{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}''' According to shrine tradition, Ch?b?zan, located about 5 km west of the shrine, is the sacred mountain of the enshrined deity Futsunushi-no-Mikoto. Ch?b?zan is also known as a candidate site of {{ill|lt=Kurefushi-no-yama|Q48753472|qid=Q48753472|1=Hodoki Mountain}} recorded in the Naka District section of the ''{{ill|lt=Hitachi no kuni fudoki|Q11481687|qid=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}}''. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, at dawn on {{ill|lt=April 12|Q2499|qid=Q2499|1=April 12}} of Y?r? 5 ({{ill|lt=721|Q23541|qid=Q23541|1=721}}), a divine light shone on Ch?b?zan. The light descended toward Fujii-go, astonishing the people, and on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|qid=Q2613|1=June 15}} they built the shrine hall. Another tradition says that during the {{ill|lt=Kash?|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} era, Futsunushi-no-Mikoto was invited from {{ill|lt=Katori Jing?|Q372380|qid=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}} by divine oracle. === Overview === It is listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' as "Fujiuchi Shrine in Naka District, Hitachi Province" and is treated as one of its candidate shrines. In K?hei 5 ({{ill|lt=1062|Q19396|qid=Q19396|1=1062}}), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|qid=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} is said to have gathered one hundred thousand troops on the northwestern ridge of this shrine on his way north, prayed here for lasting military fortune, received a branch of wisteria from before the shrine to use as a riding crop, and advanced; the place where troops were assembled is called "J?manbara." Before the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, it was called "Fujiuchi Daimy?jin." When the shrine buildings burned down in the {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|qid=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} era (1521-1528), they were rebuilt in Kan'ei 5 ({{ill|lt=1628|Q6840|qid=Q6840|1=1628}}) by {{ill|lt=Akita Sanesue|Q4701233|qid=Q4701233|1=Akita Sanesue}} of the {{ill|lt=Shishido Domain|Q3034528|qid=Q3034528|lt=Shishido domain|1=Shishido Domain}}, and rebuilt again in the Genroku era (1688-1704) by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Q1194944|qid=Q1194944|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}} of the {{ill|lt=Mito Domain|Q1146904|qid=Q1146904|1=Mito Domain}}, showing strong patronage from nearby lords. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precinct === File:Fujiuchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Fujiuchi Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival ({{ill|lt=January 7|Q2228|qid=Q2228|1=January 7}}) * Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|qid=Q2315|1=February 3}}) * Yakujin Festival ({{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|qid=Q2323|1=February 8}} and {{ill|lt=December 8|Q2301|qid=Q2301|1=December 8}}) * Annual Festival ({{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|qid=Q2502|1=April 10}}) * Rice-planting Festival ({{ill|lt=June 14|Q2612|qid=Q2612|1=June 14}}) * Nagoshi Festival ({{ill|lt=old calendar|Q11511066|qid=Q11511066|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|qid=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|qid=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Year-end Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|qid=Q2912|1=December 31}}) === Notes === {{?????}} {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province|Q11481683|qid=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} {{?? ?}} {{??????????}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=???}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Fujiuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Fujiuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:水戸市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:水戸市の建築物]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]]
